title: Cota latealata
taxonomy: Cota latealata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Anthemideae and the genus Cota. The genus Cota is a group of plants commonly known as costures, which are native to Europe and North Africa. Cota latealata is native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Mediterranean region. It is an annual or biennial herb that produces small, yellow or orange flowers.
uses: Cota latealata, also known as a type of fleabane, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine.
